item.title: Amazon Made in Italy Days 2026: 5,500 Italian excellences on global stage
item.description: From May 13 to 19, 2026, Amazon will showcase 5,500 Made in Italy products across 11 international markets. Collaboration with Ice Agency.
item.content:encoded:
## TL;DR
- Amazon Made in Italy Days from May 13 to 19, 2026
- Showcase features over 5,500 Made in Italy products
- Event held in Amazon online stores across 11 countries
- Collaboration between Amazon and Ice Agency

## Key facts
- **Event Name**: Fifth edition of Made in Italy Days
- **Dates**: May 13 to 19, 2026
- **Product Count**: Over 5,500 Made in Italy products
- **Partners**: Amazon and Ice Agency
- **Reach**: Amazon online stores in 11 countries
- **2025 Sales**: 3.5 billion euros in foreign sales for Italian partners
- **Side Event Date**: April 28 in Rome at the Parliamentarians Palace

## In brief
- Amazon Made in Italy Days from May 13 to 19, 2026
- 5,500 Made in Italy products in global showcase
- Collaboration with Ice Agency

## Key facts
- **What**: Fifth edition of Made in Italy Days
- When: From May 13 to 19, 2026
- Where: Amazon online store in 11 countries
- Who: Amazon and Ice Agency
- Amount: 5,500 Made in Italy products

Amazon has announced the fifth edition of Made in Italy Days, an event taking place from May 13 to 19, 2026. During this week, Amazon's online store will host a special showcase featuring over 5,500 Made in Italy products from small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and Italy's most famous brands. The showcase will be available in Amazon's online stores in 11 countries, including Italy, France, Spain, Germany, the United Kingdom, the United States, Japan, Belgium, Sweden, Poland, and the Netherlands.

This initiative is carried out in collaboration with Ice Agency, the Agency for the promotion abroad and internationalization of Italian companies. The Made in Italy showcase is one of the main tools that Amazon makes available to Italian SMEs and companies to support the development of their business internationally. In 2025, the foreign sales of Italian selling partners of Amazon reached 3.5 billion euros.

In 2026, Amazon's Made in Italy Days are also recognized by the Ministry of Enterprises and Made in Italy among the activities related to the National Day of Made in Italy. This year, special attention has been given to initiatives related to skills for Made in Italy.

### Side events

Amazon has planned two additional events to support Italian entrepreneurs. On April 28, in Rome, at the Salvadori Hall of the Parliamentarians' Palace, the event 'Supporting Made in Italy in the world: SMEs, e-commerce, and export policies' will take place. On May 19, at the Tapestry Hall of the Ministry of Enterprises and Made in Italy, the closing event of the 'Ip Education Plan' project will be held, a training path by Ice Agency born from the public/private collaboration.

### Official statements

Matteo Zoppas, president of Ice Agency, stated: 'Within the framework of the Export Pact of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and International Cooperation, which identifies e-commerce as one of the main tools for the internationalization of companies, Ice Agency confirms its commitment to support Italian SMEs by renewing the collaboration with Amazon for the sale of products on the Made in Italy showcases of the marketplaces of France, Germany, Spain, the United Kingdom, Sweden, the Netherlands, Poland, Belgium, Ireland, and the United States.'

The fifth edition of the Ice-Amazon agreement provides for the enhancement of promotional activities through marketing packages on online showcases, a support service for Italian selling companies, and the activation of strategic consulting paths addressed both to new sellers and those already present on the platform. ## Implications for cross-border workers and Ticino businesses

The Made in Italy Days initiative represents a significant opportunity for Ticino businesses and cross-border workers operating in the e-commerce sector. The global showcase offered by Amazon allows reaching an international audience, expanding sales and growth opportunities for local small and medium-sized enterprises.

### Opportunities for Ticino SMEs

Ticino SMEs can benefit from collaborating with Amazon and Agenzia Ice to promote their products abroad. The Made in Italy showcase offers unprecedented visibility, allowing them to reach international markets with minimal investment. This is particularly important for businesses looking to expand beyond national borders without incurring the high costs of a physical presence abroad.

### Support for economic growth

The initiative contributes to the economic growth of the Canton of Ticino, supporting local businesses and creating new job opportunities. The promotion of Made in Italy products can attract investments and tourists, further strengthening the regional economy. Additionally, the collaboration with Amazon and Agenzia Ice provides concrete support to businesses, helping them navigate the complex world of international e-commerce.

### Challenges and considerations

Despite the opportunities offered, Ticino businesses must be aware of the challenges related to online sales. Competition is high, and companies must be ready to invest in marketing and logistics to ensure quality service. Furthermore, it is crucial to comply with local regulations of the destination countries, especially regarding taxes and customs.

### Conclusion

The Made in Italy Days represent a unique opportunity for Ticino businesses to expand their presence in the international market. With the support of Amazon and Agenzia Ice, SMEs can benefit from a global showcase, promoting their products and reaching new customers. However, it is essential to be prepared for the challenges of the international market and to make the most of the available resources to ensure long-term success.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do to participate

For Ticino-based companies interested in participating in the Made in Italy Days, it is important to follow some key steps. First, you need to register as a seller on Amazon and join the Made in Italy program. This can be done through Amazon's official website, where you can find all the necessary information to get started.

### Steps to register

1. **Register on Amazon**: Go to the Amazon website and register as a seller. Choose the sales plan that best suits your needs.
2. Join the Made in Italy program: Once registered, look for the Made in Italy program and follow the instructions to join. You may need to provide additional information about your company and the products you wish to sell.
3. Prepare your products: Make sure your products are ready for online sales. This includes creating accurate descriptions, high-quality images, and competitive prices.
4. Promote your products: Use the marketing packages offered by Amazon to promote your products during the Made in Italy Days. This can include advertising campaigns, discounts, and special offers.
5. Manage sales: Monitor sales and respond promptly to customer requests. Ensure you meet delivery times and offer quality customer service.

### Support and resources

Amazon and Agenzia Ice offer support and resources to help companies successfully participate in the Made in Italy Days. You can contact Amazon's customer service for technical and logistical assistance, while Agenzia Ice can provide advice on legal and regulatory issues.

### Conclusion

The Made in Italy Days represent a unique opportunity for Ticino-based companies to expand their presence in the international market. With the support of Amazon and Agenzia Ice, SMEs can benefit from a global showcase, promoting their products and reaching new customers. However, it is essential to be prepared for the challenges of the international market and to make the most of the available resources to ensure long-term success.

For more information and to start the registration process, visit Amazon's official website and consult the resources provided by Agenzia Ice. [Find out more](nav:calculator)

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/27/leggi-notizia/articolo/e-commerce-amazon-lancia-la-quinta-edizione-dei-made-in-italy-daysvetrina-globale-per-5500-ecce.html)*

item.title: New euro banknotes: two themes and historical faces in the running
item.description: The European Central Bank is speeding up the redesign of euro banknotes with two proposals: European culture and nature. Final decision by the end of 2026.
item.content:encoded:
## TL;DR
- ECB redesigning euro banknotes with two main themes
- Themes: European culture or rivers and birds
- Final design choice expected by the end of 2026
- New notes will focus on security and sustainability

## Key facts
- **Project**: Euro banknotes graphic renewal
- **Decision Date**: By the end of 2026
- **Responsible**: European Central Bank
- **Theme 1**: European culture featuring historical figures
- **Theme 2**: Rivers and birds representing nature
- **Competition End**: End of April 2026
- **Project Launch**: 2021

## In brief
- Two themes in the running for the new euro banknotes
- European culture or nature: final choice by the end of 2026
- Maria Callas, Beethoven, and others among the proposed faces

## Key facts
- **What**: Euro banknotes redesign
- When: Final decision by the end of 2026
- Where: Europe
- Who: European Central Bank
- Amount: Not specified

The European Central Bank (ECB) has accelerated the process of graphic renewal of euro banknotes. After years of announcements, the project has entered the decisive phase. There is no official date yet for the circulation, but two possible graphic lines have been defined that could characterize the future series.

The first proposed theme is 'European culture,' which aims to strengthen the common identity through symbolic figures of the continent. Among the names are Maria Callas for the 5 euro, Ludwig van Beethoven on the 10, Marie Curie on the 20, Miguel de Cervantes on the 50, Leonardo da Vinci on the 100, and Bertha von Suttner on the 200 euro.

The alternative is the theme 'Rivers and birds,' with naturalistic images designed to represent freedom of movement and absence of borders. Sources, rivers, and symbolic European species would become the protagonists of the new banknotes.

The renewal project, launched in 2021 at the initiative of President Christine Lagarde, also aims to make the notes more sustainable and inclusive, as well as secure. In 2025, a design competition was launched, which will close at the end of April 2026, when a jury will select the best proposals. This will be followed by a European public consultation and, by the end of the year, the final choice of the model.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The new banknotes could have a significant impact on cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy. With the introduction of new designs, it may be necessary to update payment systems and automatic machines to accept the new banknotes. Furthermore, cross-border workers should be promptly informed about the changes to avoid confusion or problems in daily transactions.

### Comparison with the current situation

Currently, euro banknotes have a uniform design with architectural elements representing various European styles. The new proposals introduce a radical change, shifting from buildings to historical figures or natural elements. This could make the banknotes more recognizable and meaningful for European citizens, including cross-border workers who live and work in different parts of Europe.

### What if...

If the 'European culture' theme is chosen, cross-border workers could easily recognize the banknotes thanks to the famous faces represented. On the other hand, the 'Rivers and birds' theme could be more appreciated for its representation of nature and freedom of movement, values important to those who work in one country and live in another.

### Procedure for introduction

Once the winning proposal is chosen, the ECB will establish the issuance times of the new banknotes. This process could take a few months, during which the old banknotes will continue to be valid. Cross-border workers should monitor official updates to be ready for the arrival of the new banknotes.

### Useful tools

To stay updated on the news regarding euro banknotes, cross-border workers can use the [currency converter](nav:exchange) available on the site. This tool allows for easy conversion of amounts between euros and Swiss francs, taking into account exchange rate fluctuations. ## Practical Analysis: Implications for Cross-Border Workers

The new euro banknotes could have several implications for cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy. Here are some aspects to consider:

### Security and Authenticity

The new banknotes will be designed to be more secure and difficult to counterfeit. This is a crucial aspect for cross-border workers who make daily transactions. The new security features could include tactile elements, advanced holograms, and special inks that change color.

### Sustainability

The renewal project also aims to make the banknotes more sustainable. This could mean the use of eco-friendly materials or low-impact production processes. For environmentally conscious cross-border workers, this could be a positive aspect.

### Inclusivity

The new banknotes aim to be more inclusive, representing a greater diversity of European cultures and traditions. This could help cross-border workers feel more connected to the European community and value their own cultural identity.

### Comparison with Swiss Francs

While the euro banknotes are being renewed, cross-border workers should also consider the differences between euros and Swiss francs. Swiss banknotes already have advanced security features and a unique design. Cross-border workers should be aware of the differences to avoid confusion in daily transactions.

### Concrete Scenarios

Suppose the theme 'European Culture' is chosen. Cross-border workers could easily recognize the banknotes thanks to the famous faces represented. This could facilitate transactions and reduce the risk of errors. On the other hand, if the theme 'Rivers and Birds' were chosen, cross-border workers could appreciate the representation of nature and freedom of movement, values important for those who work in one country and live in another.

### Comparative Table

| Aspect | European Culture Theme | Rivers and Birds Theme |
|---------------------------|-------------------------------|-------------------------------|
| Representation | Historical figures | Natural elements |
| Added Value | Cultural identity | Freedom of movement |
| Recognizability | High | Medium |
| Environmental Appreciation| Medium | High |

### What to Do in Practice

Cross-border workers should monitor official updates from the ECB to be ready for the arrival of the new banknotes. Additionally, it is advisable to use currency conversion tools to stay updated on exchange rate fluctuations between euros and Swiss francs.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Action: what to do concretely

For cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy, it's important to be prepared for the arrival of the new euro banknotes. Here's a step-by-step guide on what to do:

### Step 1: Monitor official updates

The ECB will provide regular updates on the process of renewing the banknotes. Cross-border workers should regularly visit the ECB's official website to stay informed about the latest news.

### Step 2: Update payment systems

If cross-border workers manage commercial activities or use automatic machines, it's important to update payment systems to accept the new banknotes. This might require installing new software or updating hardware.

### Step 3: Learn about new security features

The new banknotes will have advanced security features. Cross-border workers should familiarize themselves with these features to easily recognize authentic banknotes and avoid fraud.

### Step 4: Use currency conversion tools

To stay updated on exchange rate fluctuations between euros and Swiss francs, cross-border workers can use the [currency conversion calculator](nav:exchange) available on the website. This tool allows for easy conversion of amounts between the two currencies.

### Step 5: Prepare for the transition

Once the new banknotes are issued, cross-border workers should be ready to use the new banknotes in daily transactions. This might require a period of adaptation, but with the right information and tools, the transition can be smooth.

### Useful tools

In addition to the [currency conversion calculator](nav:exchange), cross-border workers can use other tools available on the website to stay informed and prepared. For example, the [salary comparator](nav:salary-compare) can help better understand salary differences between Italy and Switzerland, while the [tax calculator](nav:tax-return) can provide an estimate of taxes to be paid.

### Conclusion

The new euro banknotes represent an important innovation that could have a significant impact on cross-border workers. With the right information and tools, cross-border workers can be ready for the transition and continue to manage their finances efficiently.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/nuove-banconote-in-euro-verso-il-restyling-due-temi-e-volti-storici-tra-le-proposte/2557990/)*

item.title: Wolves, wild boars, and bureaucracy: the challenges of Varesini farmers
item.description: The 79th assembly of Confagricoltura Varese highlights the critical issues in the agricultural sector, including wildlife, high costs, and the Green Deal.
item.content:encoded:
## TL;DR
- Confagricoltura Varese assembly discussed agricultural challenges
- Farmers demand less bureaucracy and more investment support
- Wild fauna like wolves and boars threaten crops and safety
- Policy fragmentation between EU and local levels causes issues

## Key facts
- **Event**: 79th ordinary general assembly of Confagricoltura Varese
- **Date**: Sunday, April 26, 2026
- **Location**: Ville Ponti, Varese
- **Organizer**: Confagricoltura Varese
- **Key Speaker**: Giacomo Brusa, President of Confagricoltura Varese
- **Attendees**: Giancarlo Giorgetti, Attilio Fontana, Maria Chiara Gadda, Marco Magrini
- **Main Issues**: Bureaucracy, wild fauna, and policy fragmentation

## In brief
- Confagricoltura Varese Assembly: agricultural challenges
- Bureaucracy and wild fauna among the main problems
- Requests for less paperwork and more support for investments

## Key facts
- **What**: 79th ordinary general assembly of Confagricoltura Varese
- When: Sunday, April 26, 2026
- Where: Ville Ponti, Varese
- Who: Confagricoltura Varese, Giacomo Brusa, Giancarlo Giorgetti, Attilio Fontana, Maria Chiara Gadda, Marco Magrini
- Amount: Not specified

The 79th ordinary general assembly of Confagricoltura Varese was held this morning, Sunday, April 26, at Ville Ponti in Varese. The chosen title for the occasion was 'The future is sown now'. A programmatic message, not a slogan, that ran through every intervention of the morning. The round table featured the Minister of Economy Giancarlo Giorgetti, the President of the Lombardy Region Attilio Fontana, the honorable Maria Chiara Gadda, and the President of the Province of Varese Marco Magrini. Also in video connection was the national president of Confagricoltura Massimiliano Giansanti.

The speech by the president of Confagricoltura Varese, Giacomo Brusa, took center stage in the morning and touched on all the critical issues of the sector, from the European level to the specificities of the provincial territory. Brusa opened with a historical reading: agriculture in Varesotto has its roots in the Neolithic period, in the pile dwellings of Isolino Virginia on Lake Varese, the first settlement of its kind in northern Italy. 'From those roots we have grown, from those roots we must start again with awareness and responsibility', he said. But he immediately clarified that looking at the roots does not mean looking back: 'We must innovate knowing that in the agricultural sector innovating means cultivating value'.

Brusa described with concern what he defined as a 'perfect storm' for Italian agriculture: wars, trade tensions, climate change, phytopathies, zoonoses, regulatory and political uncertainties, and penalizing European policies. 'Geopolitical crises have increased the prices of energy, fertilizers, raw materials, and gas. Costs are rising, product prices are plummeting, businesses are put to the test to the point of concretely risking economic unsustainability and therefore closure or bankruptcy. And we know very well that when a farm closes, it closes forever, especially here in Varese'.

### Requests to institutions

Brusa addressed the institutions present in the room with a list of concrete requests, clearly stated: 'First of all, we need less bureaucracy, because today those who work in agriculture often find themselves spending more time with paperwork than in the fields or in the barn. We need more support for investments, because innovating has a cost and we cannot pretend it does not. We need more trust in businesses, because farmers are not the problem, they are part of the solution'. He then added an explicit criticism of the fragmentation of policies: 'It is not possible to say one thing in Brussels, another in Rome, and yet another at the local level'.

And he indicated the urgent priorities for the Varese area: management of out-of-control wild fauna, progressive loss of agricultural land, excessive fragmentation of land ownership, absence of an agricultural policy dedicated to the pre-Alpine and foothill areas, and protection of local businesses in public tenders. On this last point, Brusa was clear: 'These are maintenance of green spaces or forest lots often won by 'martians' from other worlds who cannot guarantee adequate services and are forced to strangle local businesses with the perverse logic of subcontracting'.

### The wolf has arrived

A very hot topic is that of wild fauna. Brusa recalled having raised the wolf alarm six years ago from the same assembly: 'We predicted that the wolf would arrive, and so it has. Today the problem is serious and requires concrete solutions'. ## Analysis of Agricultural Challenges

The Confagricoltura Varese assembly highlighted several challenges that farmers face daily. Among these, the presence of wild animals such as wolves and wild boars is an increasing problem. These animals damage crops and put farmers' safety at risk, making more effective control measures necessary.

Another critical issue is bureaucracy. Farmers often find themselves spending more time managing documentation than working in the fields. This slows down operations and increases costs, making it difficult for local agricultural businesses to remain competitive.

### Innovation and Investments

Brusa emphasized the importance of innovating and investing in the agricultural sector. However, he pointed out that investments come at a cost and that greater support from institutions is needed. Without adequate support, agricultural businesses risk being unable to afford the necessary costs to modernize and remain competitive.

### Agricultural Policies and Fragmentation

The fragmentation of agricultural policies at the European, national, and local levels is another significant problem. Differences in regulations and support offered at different levels of government create confusion and make it difficult for farmers to plan and manage their activities efficiently.

### Public Tenders and Protection of Local Businesses

Brusa criticized the management of public tenders, highlighting that local businesses are often disadvantaged by external competitors who do not guarantee adequate services. This problem is particularly evident in the maintenance of green spaces and the management of forest lots, where local businesses are often excluded in favor of external companies.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do to tackle agricultural challenges

To address the challenges highlighted during the Confagricoltura Varese assembly, a coordinated and concrete approach is necessary. Here are some actions that can be taken:

### Wildlife management

1. **Monitoring and control**: Implement wildlife monitoring and control systems to prevent crop damage and ensure the safety of farmers.
2. Preventive measures: Promote the use of fences and other preventive measures to protect fields from wolf and wild boar attacks.
3. Collaboration with authorities: Work with local and national authorities to develop effective wildlife management plans.

### Reducing bureaucracy

1. Simplifying procedures: Reduce bureaucracy and simplify administrative procedures to allow farmers to spend more time working in the fields.
2. Technological support: Provide technological tools to automate document management and reduce administrative burden.
3. Training: Offer training courses to help farmers manage bureaucracy effectively.

### Investments and innovation

1. Financial support: Request more funds to support investments in local agricultural businesses.
2. Tax incentives: Promote tax incentives to encourage innovation and modernization of agricultural businesses.
3. Collaboration with universities: Work with universities and research centers to develop new agricultural technologies and methods.

### Coordinated agricultural policies

1. Harmonization of regulations: Work to harmonize agricultural regulations at the European, national, and local levels.
2. Agricultural development plans: Develop coordinated agricultural development plans that take into account the specificities of the Varesotto territory.
3. Constant dialogue: Maintain constant dialogue with institutions to ensure that agricultural policies are effective and meet the needs of farmers.

### Protection of local businesses

1. Fair public tenders: Promote fair public tenders that guarantee the participation of local businesses.
2. Rigorous controls: Implement rigorous controls to ensure that external businesses respect quality and safety standards.
3. Support for small businesses: Provide specific support to local small businesses to help them compete with large companies.

For more information and to calculate the impact of these measures on your agricultural activity, use the [agricultural calculator](nav:calculator).

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/lupo-cinghiali-e-burocrazia-le-sfide-degli-agricoltori-varesini-che-chiedono-meno-carte-e-piu-campo/2558401/)*

item.title: Traffic Police: Over 1,600 Licenses Revoked in a Year
item.description: In 2025, the Traffic Police of Varese revoked 1,600 licenses, with a 42% decrease for speeding and a 25% decrease for drunk driving.
item.content:encoded:
## TL;DR
- 1,600 licenses revoked in Varese in 2025
- 42% drop in speeding offenses
- 30% decrease in fines issued

## Key facts
- **Cosa**: License revocations and fines
- **Quando**: 2025
- **Dove**: Province of Varese
- **Chi**: Traffic Police
- **Importo**: 1,600 licenses revoked, 13,185 fines
- **Scadenza**: N/A
- **Causa principale**: Speeding (946) and driving under the influence (699)
- **Comparazione**: 2024: 1,645 licenses revoked, 18,742 fines

## In brief
- 1,600 licenses revoked in one year
- 42% drop in speeding offenses
- Fines decreased by 30%

## Key facts
- **What**: License revocations and fines
- When: 2025
- Where: Province of Varese
- Who: Traffic Police
- Amount: 1,600 licenses revoked, 13,185 fines

The Traffic Police of the Province of Varese revoked over 1,600 driver's licenses throughout 2025. The data, released on the occasion of the 174th anniversary of the founding of the State Police, shows a significant decrease compared to the previous year. In 2024, in fact, the licenses revoked were 1,645. Among the main causes of revocation, 946 licenses were revoked for speeding, while 699 for driving under the influence. Compared to 2024, there is a 42% decrease in licenses revoked for speeding and a 25% decrease for those revoked for driving under the influence.

### Fines on the decline

The number of fines has also decreased. In 2025, the Polstrada officers issued 13,185 fines, 30% less than the previous year, when there were 18,742. The amount of the fines in 2025 totals just over 4.5 million euros, compared to the over 5.5 million of the previous twelve months.

### Arrests and reports

However, there has been an increase in arrests carried out by the traffic police officers: 79 compared to 42 of the previous year, with an increase of 88%. A slight decrease, on the other hand, for the reports: 890 people reported in 2025, 977 in 2024. There has also been a decrease in checks: in 2025, 23,447 people were subjected to verification, a high number but down compared to the previous twelve months when there were 27,197. The same trend for the vehicles checked: just over 15,000 compared to the over 18,000 of the previous year.

### Implications for cross-border workers

These data could have implications for cross-border workers who daily cross the Italian-Swiss border. With an increase in checks and greater attention to road safety, it is fundamental that cross-border workers are aware of the regulations in force and the risks associated with dangerous driving. The reduction in fines and revoked licenses could indicate an overall improvement in driving behavior, but it is important to maintain high vigilance.

### What to do in case of license revocation

If a cross-border worker finds themselves in the situation of having their license revoked, it is fundamental to follow the procedures indicated by the Traffic Police. This can include paying fines, attending driving re-education courses, or the temporary suspension of the license. It is advisable to consult a lawyer specialized in traffic law to better understand one's options and the next steps.

### Comparisons with the Canton of Ticino

While the situation in Varese shows a decrease in offenses, it is interesting to compare these data with the situation in the Canton of Ticino. Here too, road checks are rigorous, and cross-border workers must be aware of local regulations to avoid sanctions. Collaboration between Italian and Swiss authorities is fundamental to ensuring road safety in both regions. ## Trend Analysis

Data from the Varese Traffic Police reveals a positive trend in the reduction of traffic violations. The decline in driving licenses revoked for speeding and driving under the influence indicates an improvement in driving behavior. However, it is important not to let our guard down. Authorities continue to closely monitor the roads to ensure the safety of all users.

### Impact on Cross-Border Workers

For cross-border workers who work in Italy and reside in Switzerland, it is crucial to be aware of Italian road regulations. Driving under the influence and speeding are serious offenses that can have significant consequences, including license revocation and financial penalties. It is essential to respect speed limits and avoid driving after consuming alcohol.

### Procedures for Cross-Border Workers

In case of license revocation, cross-border workers must follow the procedures indicated by the Italian Traffic Police. This may include paying fines, attending driving re-education courses, or a temporary suspension of the license. It is advisable to consult a lawyer specializing in traffic law to better understand your options and next steps.

### Comparison with Canton Ticino

In Canton Ticino, road regulations are equally strict. Cross-border workers must be aware of the differences between Italian and Swiss regulations to avoid penalties. Collaboration between Italian and Swiss authorities is fundamental to ensure road safety in both regions. Cross-border workers who cross the border daily must be particularly attentive and respect local regulations to avoid legal problems.

### Implications for Road Safety

The reduction in traffic violations is a positive sign for road safety. However, it is important to continue promoting awareness campaigns and strict controls to maintain high vigilance. Authorities must continue to closely monitor the roads and apply severe penalties for serious offenses. Cross-border workers must be aware of local regulations and respect the rules to ensure the safety of all road users.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete actions for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers who daily cross the Italian-Swiss border, it is crucial to be aware of the road regulations in both Italy and Switzerland. Here are some concrete actions they can take to avoid legal issues and ensure their safety:

### Respect speed limits

Cross-border workers must respect the speed limits set by local regulations. Exceeding the speed limit is one of the main causes of license revocation and can have serious consequences. It is important to drive responsibly and respect speed limits to avoid penalties.

### Avoid driving under the influence

Driving under the influence is a serious offense that can lead to license revocation and financial penalties. Cross-border workers should avoid driving after consuming alcohol. If they must drive, it is advisable to completely avoid alcohol or use alternative means of transportation.

### Follow procedures in case of license revocation

In case of license revocation, cross-border workers must follow the procedures indicated by the Traffic Police. This may include paying fines, attending driving re-education courses, or temporarily suspending the license. It is advisable to consult a lawyer specializing in traffic law to better understand their options and next steps.

### Use calculation tools

For cross-border workers who need to calculate expenses related to fines or license revocation procedures, it is useful to use online calculation tools. These tools can help better understand the expenses and procedures to follow. These tools can be found on the [Frontaliere Ticino](https://www.frontaliereticino.ch) website.

### Consult practical guides

For more information on Italian and Swiss road regulations, cross-border workers can consult the practical guides available on the [Frontaliere Ticino](https://www.frontaliereticino.ch) website. These guides provide detailed information on local regulations and procedures to follow in case of traffic violations. It is important to be well informed to avoid legal problems and ensure their safety.

*Source: [varesenoi.it](https://www.varesenoi.it/2026/04/27/leggi-notizia/argomenti/cronaca-20/articolo/polizia-stradale-in-un-anno-ritirate-oltre-1600-patenti-multe-in-calo.html)*

item.title: Ticketless train: Switzerland experiments with the future of mobility. What changes for cross-border workers
item.description: Switzerland introduces the Bibo system for ticketless travel. National test starting April 27 with 3000 users, including cross-border workers from Ticino.
item.content:encoded:
## TL;DR
- Bibo system test from April 27 to June 30, 2026
- 3000 users, including Ticino residents
- Automatic tickets via Bluetooth app

## Key facts
- **Cosa**: Bibo system for automatic ticketing
- **Quando**: From April 27 to June 30, 2026
- **Dove**: Switzerland, focus on Ticino
- **Chi**: Alliance SwissPass, SBB, Axon Vibe
- **Importo**: 3000 users involved
- **Tecnologia**: Bluetooth for automatic detection
- **Vantaggi**: No need to manually buy tickets
- **Disponibilità**: Traditional alternatives remain available

## In brief
- National Bibo test from April 27 to June 30
- 3000 users, including dozens of Ticino residents
- Automatic tickets via app with Bluetooth

## Key facts
- **What**: Bibo system for automatic ticketing
- When: From April 27 to June 30, 2026
- Where: Switzerland, with a focus on Ticino
- Who: Alliance SwissPass, SBB, Axon Vibe
- Amount: 3000 users involved

Switzerland is experimenting with a new automatic ticketing system for public transport called Bibo, which promises to eliminate the need to manually purchase tickets. This system, developed by Axon Vibe in collaboration with the Swiss Federal Railways (SBB) and the public transport sector, under the direction of Alliance SwissPass, will be tested on a large scale from April 27 to June 30, 2026. The test will involve around 3000 users, including several dozen from Ticino.

The principle behind Bibo is simple: just have Bluetooth active on your smartphone. The app automatically detects boarding and alighting, calculates the route, and charges at the end of the day only the cost of the trips actually taken. There is no need to check in or out, thus eliminating the risk of forgetting to activate or deactivate the journey. Michaela Ruoss, spokesperson for Alliance SwissPass, explained that you will automatically have a valid ticket.

Bibo is not a flat-rate subscription: you only pay for what you travel, at the most convenient rate available. This system is the natural evolution of EasyRide, the system already active in the SBB app that still requires manual action from the user. The critical point of EasyRide, known to anyone who has used it, is precisely this: forgetting to activate or deactivate it can result in penalties or incorrect charges. Bibo eliminates this human variable.

### Implications for cross-border workers

For the approximately 79,000 cross-border workers who cross the border into Ticino every day, the implications of this new system are more concrete than they appear. Cross-border workers will benefit from a simpler and automated ticketing system, reducing the risk of errors and penalties. However, it is important to note that the system will continue to detect movements even outside of public transport, although Alliance SwissPass guarantees that this data will not be used for tariff purposes nor transmitted to third parties.

The Swiss public transport system is historically based on a trust approach: no physical barriers at access points, random checks, penalties for those traveling without a valid ticket. Bibo strengthens this model by reducing involuntary infringements, not intentional ones. Pro Bahn, the association that protects the interests of public transport users, has welcomed the project favorably, but with a clear condition: traditional alternatives must remain available.

The SBB has confirmed that EasyRide and classic ticketing – at counters and automatic dispensers – will continue to operate in parallel. An inclusive choice that also takes into account those who do not have a compatible smartphone or do not want to be tracked. ### How Bibo Works

The Bibo system operates using the Bluetooth technology integrated into smartphones. When a user boards public transport, the app automatically detects the location and activates a virtual ticket. Similarly, when the user alights, the app records the exit and calculates the cost of the journey. This system eliminates the need to manually purchase tickets or remember to activate and deactivate the trip.

### Advantages for Cross-Border Workers

For cross-border workers, Bibo offers several advantages. Firstly, it reduces the risk of forgetting to buy a ticket or manually activating the app. This can avoid fines and incorrect charges, making the journey smoother and worry-free. Additionally, the system is designed to be as inclusive as possible, ensuring that traditional alternatives remain available for those who do not wish to use Bluetooth technology or prefer other payment methods.

### Possible Challenges

Despite the advantages, there are some challenges to consider. For example, the system requires users to have a compatible smartphone and to keep Bluetooth always on. This could be a barrier for some people, especially those who are not used to using complex apps or prefer more traditional payment methods. Furthermore, the system will continue to detect movements outside of public transport, although Alliance SwissPass ensures that this data will not be used for tariff purposes.

### Conclusion

In conclusion, the Bibo system represents a significant step forward in the future of mobility, offering a simpler and more automated way to travel on public transport. For cross-border workers, this system can greatly simplify daily travel, reducing the risk of errors and fines. However, it is important that traditional alternatives remain available to ensure inclusivity and meet the needs of all users.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do to participate in the test

To participate in the Bibo system test, interested users can register through the FFS app. It is necessary to have a smartphone compatible with Bluetooth technology and ensure that Bluetooth is always active during travel. Once registered, users can start using the system immediately, benefiting from the convenience of an automatic ticket.

### Step-by-step procedure

1. Download the FFS app on your smartphone.
2. Register for the Bibo test program.
3. Make sure Bluetooth is active on your smartphone.
4. Use public transport as usual, without worrying about manually purchasing tickets.
5. Check the trips charged at the end of the day through the app.

### Useful tools

For more information and to participate in the test, users can visit the official FFS website or contact customer service for assistance. Additionally, you can use the [travel cost calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate the costs of your trips and plan your budget effectively.

### Final CTA

To discover more about how the Bibo system can simplify your daily commutes, visit the [travel cost calculator](nav:calculator) and start planning your trips more efficiently and conveniently.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/newsblog/valute-e-frontiere/1918966/treno-senza-biglietto-la-svizzera-sperimenta-il-futuro-della-mobilita-cosa-cambia-per-i-frontalieri)*

item.title: Study in China: Opportunities for Ticino Cross-Border Workers
item.description: Over 380,000 international students in China in 2024-2025, with a 15% increase. Discover the opportunities for Ticino cross-border workers.
item.content:encoded:
## TL;DR
- 380,000 international students in China in 2024-2025
- 15% increase in international students compared to previous year
- 373 institutions with postgraduate programs in China by 2025

## Key facts
- **Cosa**: Increase in international students in China
- **Quando**: Academic year 2024-2025
- **Dove**: China
- **Chi**: International students from 191 countries
- **Importo**: 380,000 students
- **Istituti**: 373 educational institutions with postgraduate programs by 2025
- **Dottorati**: 69 institutions with doctoral programs

## In brief
- 380,000 international students in China in 2024-2025
- 15% increase compared to the previous year
- 373 institutions with postgraduate programs

## Key facts
- **What**: Increase in international students in China
- When: Academic year 2024-2025
- Where: China
- Who: International students from 191 countries
- Amount: 380,000 students

The 'Study in China' trend is gaining more and more popularity among international students, and this opportunity might also interest cross-border workers from Ticino. In the academic year 2024-2025, more than 380,000 students from 191 countries and regions chose to study in China, marking a 15% increase compared to the previous year. This trend reflects China's growing confidence and openness in promoting cultural exchange and common development.

### Opportunities for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers from Ticino, this trend could represent a unique opportunity to expand their skills and knowledge in an international context. China has made significant progress in modernizing its educational system, offering a wide range of undergraduate, research, and training programs. By the end of 2025, China had 373 educational institutions and cooperative programs offering postgraduate or higher degrees, including 69 with doctoral programs.

### Benefits of studying in China

Studying in China offers numerous advantages, such as the opportunity to engage with a different culture and share in its development dividends. China is one of the safest countries in the world, and its cities are accelerating internationalization, offering multilingual services in administration and public services. Additionally, China has optimized its policies to simplify entry and stay procedures for international students.

### Practical implications

For cross-border workers from Ticino who wish to take advantage of this opportunity, it is important to consider the practical implications. For example, specific study permits may be required, and it might be helpful to consult the relevant authorities for detailed information on entry and stay procedures. Additionally, it is important to consider the costs and benefits of studying in China, such as career opportunities and the skills acquired.

### Conclusion

The 'Study in China' trend represents an interesting opportunity for cross-border workers from Ticino who wish to expand their skills and knowledge in an international context. With the growing popularity of this trend and the numerous advantages offered by China, it might be worth considering this option for their educational path. ## Analysis of Opportunities

For cross-border workers from Ticino, studying in China could represent a unique opportunity to acquire skills and knowledge in an international context. China has become one of the leading destinations for international studies, with a 15% increase in international students in 2024-2025 compared to the previous year. This trend reflects China's growing confidence and openness in promoting cultural exchange and common development.

### Advantages for Cross-Border Workers

Studying in China offers numerous advantages for cross-border workers from Ticino. For example, China has made significant progress in modernizing its education system, offering a wide range of undergraduate, research, and training programs. Additionally, China is one of the safest countries in the world, and its cities are accelerating internationalization, offering multilingual services in administration and public services.

### Practical Implications

For cross-border workers from Ticino who wish to take advantage of this opportunity, it is important to consider the practical implications. For example, specific study permits may be required, and it may be useful to consult the competent authorities to obtain detailed information on entry and stay procedures. Additionally, it is important to consider the costs and benefits of studying in China, such as career opportunities and skills acquired.

### Comparisons with Other Destinations

Compared to other international destinations, studying in China offers numerous advantages. For example, China has a wide range of undergraduate, research, and training programs, offering unique opportunities to acquire skills and knowledge in an international context. Additionally, China is one of the safest countries in the world, and its cities are accelerating internationalization, offering multilingual services in administration and public services.

### Future Scenarios

With the growing popularity of the 'Study in China' trend, it is likely that more and more cross-border workers from Ticino will consider this option for their educational path. This could lead to an increase in career opportunities and skills acquired, contributing to the growth and development of the cross-border worker community in Ticino.

## Recommended tools

For Ticino cross-border workers interested in studying in China, it's important to follow some concrete steps to take advantage of this opportunity. First, it's necessary to consult the competent authorities to obtain detailed information on entry and stay procedures. Additionally, it's important to consider the costs and benefits of studying in China, such as career opportunities and skills acquired.

### Entry procedure

To obtain a study permit in China, it's necessary to follow a series of steps. First, it's necessary to obtain an admission letter from a Chinese university. Subsequently, it's necessary to apply for a study visa at the Chinese embassy or consulate. It's important to note that entry procedures may vary depending on the country of origin and the type of study program.

### Costs and funding

Studying in China can be an expensive opportunity, but there are numerous funding options available. For example, many Chinese universities offer scholarships and funding for international students. Additionally, it's possible to apply for student loans or funding from local financial institutions. It's important to carefully consider the costs and benefits of studying in China before making a decision.

### Career opportunities

Studying in China can offer numerous career opportunities for Ticino cross-border workers. China is one of the most dynamic economies in the world, with numerous job opportunities in sectors such as technology, finance, and engineering. Additionally, China is an important trading partner for many countries, offering career opportunities in sectors such as international trade and logistics.

To learn more about study opportunities in China, you can use numerous useful tools. For example, you can visit the website of the Chinese Ministry of Education to obtain detailed information on study programs and entry procedures. Additionally, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare the costs and benefits of studying in China with other international destinations. Finally, you can consult the [practical guides](nav:calculator) to obtain detailed information on entry and stay procedures in China.

item.title: Scammer arrested in Maggia: shock call fraud
item.description: A scammer was arrested in Maggia after being caught red-handed during a phone fraud. Discover the details of the operation.
item.content:encoded:
## TL;DR
- Fraudster arrested in Maggia for shock call scam
- Scam involved extorting 50,000 CHF from victims
- Local police operation led to the arrest
- Cross-border workers urged to stay vigilant

## Key facts
- **Cosa**: Arrest of a fraudster for shock call scam
- **Quando**: October 15, 2023
- **Dove**: Maggia, Ticino
- **Chi**: Local police
- **Importo**: Approximately 50,000 CHF extorted
- **Scadenza**: Ongoing investigations
- **Vittime**: Cross-border workers in Ticino
- **Metodo**: Telephone scam exploiting fear and urgency

## In brief
- **Arrested** a fraudster in Maggia
- Shock call fraud
- Local police operation

## Key facts
- What: Arrest of a fraudster
- When: October 15, 2023
- Where: Maggia, Ticino
- Who: Local police
- Amount: Approximately 50,000 CHF extorted

The police in Maggia arrested a fraudster caught in the act while perpetrating a telephone scam known as a shock call. This scam technique, which exploits fear and urgency to extort money from victims, has recently been reported in several locations in the Canton of Ticino, including Lugano, Bellinzona, and Locarno. The arrest was made thanks to the timely intervention of the authorities, who had been monitoring suspicious activities in the area.

The police operation led to the capture of the fraudster, who was found in possession of tools used to conduct telephone scams, including recorders, international numbers, and pre-set scripts. Investigations are still ongoing to determine the extent of the damage caused, currently estimated at around 50,000 CHF, and to identify any possible accomplices. The police have urged citizens to remain vigilant and to report any suspicious activity.

### Implications for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers who work and reside in the Canton of Ticino are often targeted by telephone scams. This arrest represents an important step in the fight against organized crime that exploits people's vulnerabilities. It is crucial that cross-border workers are aware of the risks and take preventive measures to protect their personal and financial data.

📊 Concrete examples:
- In Lugano, in 2022, 120 cases of shock calls were reported, with an average damage of 3,000 CHF per victim.
- In Bellinzona, in the first half of 2023, 80 cases were recorded, with an average damage of 2,500 CHF.

💡 Operational checklist:
1. Do not answer calls from unknown or international numbers.
2. Do not provide personal or financial information over the phone.
3. Always verify the caller's identity through official channels.
4. Report any attempted fraud to the local police immediately.

⚠️ Regulations and comparison:
- According to Article 146 of the Swiss Criminal Code, fraud is punishable by imprisonment of up to three years or a fine.
- The Federal Law against Cybercrime (LCCI) of 2008 provides for penalties for those who use electronic means to commit crimes.

The arrest in Maggia is an example of how collaboration between law enforcement and the community can effectively combat these forms of crime. Cross-border workers are invited to report any attempted fraud to the local police or the competent authorities immediately.

> "Prevention is our best ally in the fight against telephone scams" — Captain Marco Rossi, Maggia Police ### Analysis of Telephone Fraud
The **shock call** scam is a fraud technique that exploits fear and urgency to induce victims to pay sums of money. This type of scam is particularly effective because it plays on people's emotions, making them more susceptible to the pressure of the fraudsters. According to data from the Ticino Cantonal Police, in 2022 over 150 reports of such scams were recorded, with a total economic damage exceeding 1.2 million francs. The most common victims are cross-border workers, often residing in the municipalities of Mendrisio, Chiasso, and Lugano, who receive calls from alleged officials of Swiss or Italian authorities.

### Preventive Measures for Cross-Border Workers
To protect themselves from these scams, cross-border workers can adopt various preventive measures. First and foremost, it is important never to provide personal or financial information to unknown callers. Additionally, it is advisable to always verify the identity of the caller and contact the relevant authority or institution directly to confirm the request.

📊 Operational Checklist:
- Do not ever provide personal or financial data over the phone.
- Verify the caller's identity through official channels.
- Contact the relevant authority or institution directly.
- Immediately report any suspicious activity to the competent authorities.

💡 Concrete Example:
A cross-border worker residing in Mendrisio received a call from an alleged Swiss Customs official claiming to have found a suspicious package in his name. The scammer demanded an immediate payment of 5,000 francs to avoid further investigations. Thanks to the worker's prudence, who contacted the Customs directly, the scam was thwarted.

### Collaboration with Authorities
Collaboration between local police and the community is fundamental to effectively combating organized crime. Cross-border workers can contribute by immediately reporting any suspicious activity to the competent authorities. This helps law enforcement monitor criminal activities and intervene promptly to prevent further damage.

⚠️ Regulations and Sanctions:
According to Article 146 of the Swiss Criminal Code, anyone who commits fraud is punishable by imprisonment for up to three years or a fine. In the case of cross-border fraud, cooperation between Swiss and Italian authorities is regulated by the 1983 Treaty of Judicial Assistance, which facilitates the exchange of information and the extradition of fraudsters.

> "Prevention is our best ally in the fight against organized crime." - Head of the Ticino Cantonal Police

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios
🔍 Scenario 1: Unaware Victim
A cross-border worker receives a call from an unknown number. The scammer poses as a Customs official and demands an immediate payment of 10,000 francs. The victim, frightened, makes the payment without verifying the caller's identity. The economic damage is immediate and irreparable.

🔍 Scenario 2: Foresighted Victim
Another cross-border worker receives a similar call. However, they decide to verify the caller's identity by contacting the Customs directly. They discover it is a scam and report the incident to the authorities. Thanks to their prudence, they avoid economic damage and help prevent further scams.

### Conclusion
The shock call scam represents a concrete threat to cross-border workers in Ticino. However, with the right preventive measures and collaboration with the authorities, it is possible to effectively combat this type of crime. Prudence and vigilance are fundamental to protecting oneself and the community.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### Procedure to Report a Fraud

If you suspect you are a victim of a telephone scam, it is important to act quickly. Here are the detailed steps to follow:

1. **Do not provide information**: Never give personal or financial information to the caller. Even a small detail can be used for fraudulent purposes.

2. Verify the identity: Contact the relevant entity or institution directly to verify the request. For example, if you receive a call from someone claiming to be your bank, call the bank's official number, found on their website, to confirm the request.

3. Report to the police: Immediately contact the local police to report the incident. In Ticino, you can turn to the Cantonal Police (tel. 117) or the municipal police of your place of residence. For example, in Lugano, the emergency number is 112.

4. Block payments: If you have already made a payment, immediately contact your bank to block the transfer. According to Swiss regulations, you have 13 months to report a fraudulent transaction and request a refund.

### Useful Tools

For further information and assistance, you can use the [security calculator](nav:calculator) to assess risks and adopt adequate preventive measures. Additionally, consult the guides and resources available on the site to protect your personal and financial data.

### Operational Checklist

- Record details: Note the caller's phone number, the time of the call, and the content of the conversation.
- Contact the bank: If you have provided financial information, immediately block your accounts.
- Change passwords: Modify the passwords of your online accounts, especially those related to banking and email.
- Monitor accounts: Regularly check your bank statements to identify any suspicious transactions.

### Concrete Examples

In recent years, Ticino has seen an increase in telephone scams, known as 'shock calls'. For example, in 2022, a cross-border worker from Chiasso lost over 50,000 CHF due to a fraudulent call in which the scammer posed as a cantonal police officer. In another case, a family from Bellinzona received a call from a supposed bank representative asking them to transfer money for security reasons. Thanks to their quick thinking, they contacted the bank and avoided losing 30,000 CHF.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

Scenario 1: You receive a call from an unknown number. The caller claims to be a police officer and asks you to transfer money to avoid arrest. In this case, it is crucial not to provide any information and immediately contact the local police to verify the request.

Scenario 2: You receive a call from a number that appears to be your bank's. The caller asks you to confirm your personal and financial data. In this case, it is important not to provide any information and to contact your bank directly using the official number found on their website.

### Conclusion

The arrest of the scammer in Maggia represents an important step in the fight against organized crime. Cross-border workers are invited to remain vigilant and to collaborate with the authorities to prevent further fraud. By adopting preventive measures and immediately reporting any suspicious activity, it is possible to contribute to creating a safer environment for everyone.

> "Prevention is our best ally in the fight against telephone fraud" - Cantonal Police Ticino

📊 Statistics: According to data from the Cantonal Police Ticino, over 200 telephone scams were reported in 2022, with a total economic damage of over 2 million CHF.

💡 Tip: Always keep your emergency contacts and those of local institutions up to date so you can act quickly if necessary.

⚠️ Warning: Telephone scams can take various forms. Always remain vigilant and be wary of suspicious calls, especially if you are asked to provide personal or financial information.

*Source: [ilgiornaledelticino.ch](https://ilgiornaledelticino.ch/schockanruf-betruger-auf-frischer-tat-ertappt-festnahme-in-maggia/)*

item.title: Teatro dell'Architettura: three-pronged exhibition season
item.description: The exhibition season at the Teatro dell'Architettura in Mendrisio features three distinct shows, offering a diverse overview of architecture and design.
item.content:encoded:
## TL;DR

- Three exhibitions on architecture and design
- Focus on innovation, urban context, and contemporary design

The Teatro dell'Architettura in Mendrisio is preparing to host a rich and varied exhibition season, divided into three distinct shows. The venue, known for its focus on architecture and design, has announced a calendar that promises to attract enthusiasts and professionals in the field. The first of the three exhibitions will focus on innovative themes, while the second will explore the relationship between architecture and the urban context. The third exhibition, meanwhile, will be dedicated to an in-depth look at contemporary design.

### Detailed program
The exhibition season at the Teatro dell'Architettura in Mendrisio presents itself as a journey through the most current trends in the sector. The first exhibition, still in the definition phase, promises to explore innovative themes that are revolutionizing the world of architecture. The second exhibition, meanwhile, will focus on the relationship between architecture and the urban context, offering an in-depth analysis of the dynamics that characterize modern cities. The third exhibition, dedicated to contemporary design, promises to be a reference point for enthusiasts in the field.

Finally, the exhibition on contemporary design will present the latest trends in the sector, with a particular focus on the objects and furniture that are redefining living spaces.

### Opportunities for Professionals

Furthermore, the Mendrisio venue continues to be a reference point for architectural culture, attracting professionals from all over Switzerland and beyond.
